# Review Checklist

Use this checklist before finalizing significant frontend changes.
This is the final gate before marking work as done.

---

## How to Use

Go through each section. Check every item that applies to your change.
If an item doesn't apply, skip it — but be honest about whether it actually doesn't apply or you're avoiding work.

---

## 1. Semantic HTML

- [ ] Interactive elements use the correct native element (`button`, `a`, `select`, `input`)
- [ ] Page structure uses landmark elements (`main`, `nav`, `header`, `footer`, `section`)
- [ ] Heading hierarchy is logical and doesn't skip levels
- [ ] Lists use `ol`, `ul`, or `dl` as appropriate
- [ ] Tables use `thead`, `tbody`, `th` with `scope`, and `caption`
- [ ] Forms use `label`, `fieldset`, and `legend` correctly
- [ ] `div` and `span` are only used for layout/styling, not as interactive or semantic substitutes

**Reference:** `references/semantic-html.md`

---

## 2. Accessibility

- [ ] Every interactive element is reachable and operable with keyboard
- [ ] Focus indicators are visible (`:focus-visible` is styled)
- [ ] Every form input has a visible, associated `label`
- [ ] Icon-only buttons have `aria-label`
- [ ] Decorative images have `alt=""`; informative images have descriptive `alt`
- [ ] Error messages use `role="alert"` and are linked via `aria-describedby`
- [ ] Dynamic content changes use appropriate live regions
- [ ] Dialogs trap focus and return it on close
- [ ] Color is never the sole indicator of meaning
- [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion` is respected for animations

**Reference:** `references/accessibility.md`

---

## 3. TypeScript

- [ ] No `any` types
- [ ] No unsafe casts (`as Type`) without justification
- [ ] Props are typed with interfaces or type aliases
- [ ] Nullable and optional values are handled explicitly
- [ ] API responses are validated at the boundary (zod, etc.) or typed from a shared schema
- [ ] Discriminated unions are used for state that has distinct modes
- [ ] Event handlers are typed (`React.FormEvent`, `React.ChangeEvent`, etc.)
- [ ] Generic components have correct type inference

**Reference:** `references/typescript-strict.md`

---

## 4. UI States

- [ ] **Loading** state shows skeleton or spinner appropriate to the context
- [ ] **Empty** state has a message and, where relevant, a call to action
- [ ] **Error** state explains what went wrong and offers retry where possible
- [ ] **Success** state confirms the user's action (inline or toast)
- [ ] **Disabled** state is visually clear and accessible
- [ ] **Submitting** state disables the submit button and shows progress
- [ ] **Validation** feedback appears at the right time (on submit, on blur, etc.)
- [ ] **Optimistic updates** revert correctly on failure (if used)

**Reference:** `references/ui-states.md`

---

## 5. Repo Conventions

- [ ] File naming matches the project's pattern (PascalCase, kebab-case, etc.)
- [ ] Component location matches the project's directory structure
- [ ] Styling uses the project's existing approach (Tailwind, CSS modules, styled-components, etc.)
- [ ] State management uses the project's existing patterns
- [ ] Data fetching uses the project's existing libraries
- [ ] Form handling matches existing form patterns
- [ ] Testing follows the project's existing test style and tooling

---

## 6. Maintainability

- [ ] Components have a single, clear responsibility
- [ ] No premature abstractions — helpers are only created for repeated patterns
- [ ] Data flow is simple and traceable (props down, events up)
- [ ] Side effects are explicit and minimal
- [ ] No dead code, unused imports, or commented-out blocks
- [ ] Another engineer could understand this code without asking the author

---

## 7. Security

- [ ] No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` without sanitization (DOMPurify or equivalent)
- [ ] User-generated content is escaped in rendering — no raw HTML injection
- [ ] External links use `rel="noopener noreferrer"` with `target="_blank"`
- [ ] URL parameters and query strings are validated before use
- [ ] No secrets, API keys, or tokens in client-side code
- [ ] Form actions validate and sanitize server-side, not just client-side
- [ ] Third-party scripts are loaded with `integrity` or from trusted sources
- [ ] `href` and `src` attributes are not interpolated from untrusted input without validation

**Reference:** `references/security.md`

---

## 8. Responsive Design

- [ ] Layout works on mobile (320px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px+)
- [ ] No horizontal scrolling on any viewport
- [ ] Touch targets are at least 44x44px on mobile
- [ ] Text is readable without zooming (minimum 16px base font)
- [ ] Content reflows correctly at 200% zoom
- [ ] Long text, dynamic content, and edge cases don't break layout
- [ ] Images and media scale correctly and don't overflow containers
- [ ] Navigation is usable on mobile (hamburger, bottom nav, etc.)

**Reference:** `references/responsive.md`

---

## 9. Internationalization Readiness

- [ ] No hardcoded user-facing strings (use constants, i18n keys, or CMS content)
- [ ] Dates, numbers, and currencies use `Intl` formatters, not manual string building
- [ ] Plurals use ICU MessageFormat, not string concatenation
- [ ] Layout doesn't break with longer translated text (German, Finnish are ~30–40% longer)
- [ ] No fixed widths on text containers — design for text expansion
- [ ] No text embedded in images
- [ ] `lang` attribute is set on `<html>` or per-section where language changes
- [ ] CSS uses logical properties (`inline-start`, `inline-end`) instead of `left`/`right`
- [ ] Text direction is handled if RTL languages are in scope (`dir="auto"` or `dir="rtl"`)

**Reference:** `references/i18n.md`

---

## 10. Bundle Impact

- [ ] New dependencies are justified — checked size on bundlephobia.com
- [ ] Heavy libraries are kept in Server Components or code-split where possible
- [ ] No duplicate functionality — check if the project already has a utility for this
- [ ] Tree-shakeable imports are used (`import { x } from "lib/x"` vs `import lib from "lib"`)
- [ ] `"use client"` boundaries are intentional — each one adds to the client bundle

---

## 11. Testing

- [ ] Tests cover user-facing behavior, not implementation details
- [ ] Async flows (loading, error, success) are tested
- [ ] Form validation (valid and invalid input) is tested
- [ ] Accessibility is tested (roles, labels, keyboard interaction)
- [ ] API calls are mocked at the network level (MSW) not by mocking fetch
- [ ] Test data uses factories, not brittle static fixtures
- [ ] No snapshot tests for full component trees
- [ ] Tests are independent — no shared mutable state between tests

**Reference:** `references/testing.md` (React), `references/testing-vue.md` (Vue/Nuxt)

---

## 12. CSS and Styling

- [ ] Styling approach is consistent with the project (Tailwind, CSS Modules, scoped, etc.)
- [ ] Design tokens / CSS custom properties are used for colors, spacing, typography
- [ ] No hardcoded color values, font sizes, or spacing
- [ ] No `!important` except in utility classes
- [ ] No nesting deeper than 3 levels
- [ ] Theme (light/dark) is tested if applicable
- [ ] Transitions specify properties explicitly (not `transition: all`)
- [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion` is respected for animations

**Reference:** `references/css-architecture.md`

---

## 13. Performance (When Relevant)

- [ ] Heavy components are code-split (React.lazy, defineAsyncComponent, @defer, dynamic import)
- [ ] Expensive computations are cached (useMemo, computed, $derived)
- [ ] Lists with many items use virtualization (if the project supports it)
- [ ] Images use appropriate formats, sizes, lazy loading, and explicit dimensions
- [ ] Fonts use `font-display: swap` and are preloaded or self-hosted
- [ ] No unnecessary re-renders or deep reactivity tracking
- [ ] LCP image is prioritized (priority, preload, fetchpriority)
- [ ] Third-party scripts use appropriate loading strategy

**Reference:** `references/performance.md` (React/Next.js), `references/performance-vue.md` (Vue/Nuxt)

---

## Final Check

Before marking the task as complete:

1. Re-read the original requirement. Did you build what was asked?
2. Test the golden path — does the main flow work?
3. Test edge cases — empty data, errors, rapid clicks, long text.
4. Check the diff — is every change intentional? No leftover debugging code?
5. Would you be comfortable handing this code to another engineer to maintain?
